paper_title: Geological Characterization of the 3D Seismic Record within the Gas Bearing Upper Miocene Sediments in the Northern Part of the Bjelovar Subdepression—Application of Amplitude Versus Offset Analysis and Artificial Neural Network

methods.description: This method involves analyzing seismic attributes derived from seismic data to enhance the interpretation of subsurface structures and identify potential hydrocarbon reservoirs.

steps.description: Derive seismic attributes from post-stack and pre-stack data, including envelope amplitude, instantaneous phase, instantaneous frequency, and apparent polarity.
steps.searchable_summary: derive seismic attributes from seismic data

steps.description: Analyze derived seismic attributes to obtain detailed reservoir characterization and quantification.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze seismic attributes for reservoir characterization

methods.description: This method uses AVO analysis to study the variation in seismic reflection amplitude with offset or angle, which helps in identifying hydrocarbon indicators.

steps.description: Transform data from the offset domain to the angle domain to calculate intercept and gradient using linear regression.
steps.searchable_summary: transform offset data to angle domain for AVO analysis

steps.description: Calculate AVO intercept and gradient attributes using linear regression on angle domain data.
Generate secondary AVO attributes such as the product of intercept and gradient, and Sign(intercept) × gradient.
Create AVO crossplots to represent AVO data and identify trends such as the fluid line.
steps.searchable_summary: create AVO crossplots for data visualization
